rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to invalid identifiers or mismatched types in the same line and fix or remove the offending clause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts; avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified identifiers.
In implementation scopes, use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables; do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only identifiers visible in the current scope; avoid undefined or external references.
AGREE type declarations must match AADL scalar types: int, real, bool; avoid invalid or unresolved type names.
When type mismatches occur, align helper declarations with the expression types: bool for comparisons, int for integers, real for reals.
Fix or remove AGREE clauses that reference unresolved identifiers or have invalid types rather than adding new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare types explicitly using int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assign for implementation-level bindings.
Do not attempt to resolve type errors by adding new architecture; instead, correct or remove the invalid clause.
